lane: operator-directed (P0; scoped to existing/ready surfaces; service_state represented as deferred not forced)
doctrine: Absence becomes declared, not laundered. The manifest surfaces gaps; it does not force undecided work into existence.
built: coverage/manifest — machine-readable: category|name|status|evidence|rationale. 8 claim kinds + 5 active probes + 4 lab-backed backends (implemented/lab_backed) + service_state (deferred -> preflights/SERVICE_STATE.md).
scripts/check-coverage-manifest.sh — fail-closed checker; CI job coverage-manifest.
fails_closed_when: an implemented surface is missing from the manifest: every ClaimKind enum variant, every nq-monitor *_probe.rs, every tests/fixtures/<dir> (silent absence)
an entry references dead/unknown evidence (path missing)
a deferred/not_expected entry has no rationale, or an unknown status
scope_decision: NQ-repo surfaces ONLY. nq-witness profiles (zfs/smart/fs_inode/kea_dhcp) are that repo's concern — nq CI does not check out the sibling, so referencing them would be a dead-evidence false-positive. service_state present as deferred (not forced).
distinct_from: runtime substrate-coverage declaration (SUBSTRATE_COVERAGE_DECLARATION_GAP — what hosts/services are expected to be OBSERVED) is a separate 'coverage' notion, still candidate. This is dev/impl coverage.

verify.three_failure_modes_proven: dead-evidence -> exit 1; dropped claim_kind -> exit 1; deferred-without-rationale -> exit 1 (scratch-copy meta-proof)
verify.gates: gap-status / receipt-gate / witness-boundaries green; no Rust changed (full suite unaffected, CI runs it)

acceptance: P0 #2 done: machine-readable + CI-checked manifest; prevents silent absence; service_state declared deferred pointing to the breadcrumb. Loop AUDIT/idle. PUSH: held (workweek). P0 status: #1 deferred (schema slice), #2 done, #3 done. Remaining: live-wire Kea control-socket (gated real integration), and the deferred service_state schema slice — both operator-gated.
